busulfan.Normal Function: Standard busulfan dosing for conditioning regimen. Use targeted AUC-based dosing with therapeutic drug monitoring.
busulfan.Intermediate Function: Standard dosing with TDM. Moderately reduced detoxification capacity.
busulfan.No Function (Null Genotype): Reduced detoxification of busulfan metabolites. Associated with increased risk of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS/VOD) in HSCT conditioning. Consider dose reduction or alternative conditioning. Aggressive TDM recommended.

oxaliplatin.Normal Function: Standard oxaliplatin dosing per protocol. Standard detoxification of platinum metabolites.
oxaliplatin.No Function (Null Genotype): GSTM1 null associated with reduced platinum detoxification and increased neurotoxicity risk. Monitor for peripheral neuropathy. Some data suggest improved response in colorectal cancer (reduced drug inactivation) — risk-benefit discussion warranted.

cisplatin.Normal Function: Standard cisplatin dosing. Normal glutathione conjugation of platinum.
cisplatin.No Function (Null Genotype): GSTT1 null associated with increased ototoxicity risk in pediatric patients. Audiologic monitoring essential. GSTM1 null may correlate with improved tumor response but increased nephrotoxicity.

cyclophosphamide.No Function (Null Genotype): GSTM1/GSTT1 null associated with altered cyclophosphamide metabolism. Increased acrolein accumulation risk (hemorrhagic cystitis). Ensure adequate mesna prophylaxis.
